_comment: Shared mock data for both Shoka-shape framework prototypes (React + Svelte). Identical content on both sides so the operator's comparison is about how each framework PRESENTS the data, not the data itself. Hardcoded; no fetch. Tree: namespace/project/path. Paths use forward slashes; 1-2 levels of nesting.

files.content:
# Shoka — maintenance

This project tracks issues, directives, and reports for **Shoka itself**.

- `backlog.md` — running list of issues (stable `B-NN` ids).
- `directives/` — work orders, one per task.
- `reports/` — completion + progress reports.

> Shoka is a Markdown-MCP backend: project docs exposed over MCP, filesystem-isolated by `namespace/projectName`, version-controlled per write.

# Maintenance Backlog

| id | status | summary |
|----|--------|---------|
| B-04 | active | Pre-commit hook crashes the agent renderer |
| B-18 | fixed | Write-path stalls under concurrency (global lock) |
| B-25 | active | Project state goes stale after out-of-band commit |
| B-27 | draft | Mechanical prevention of `--no-verify` bypass |

Items are append-mostly; superseded entries are marked, never deleted.

# Maintenance Journal

Append-only narrative log of patterns noticed during maintenance.

## Framing-bound investigation

Each artefact inherits the previous one's framing. The fix is the same shape at every level: **state the frame, then verify it** — source against reports, live operation against framings, target-backward reasoning against the current stack.

# Storage redesign

**status:** active

Remove the global `FSGitStorage` mutex. `read_file` becomes `os.ReadFile` + content-etag (no git walk); writes hold a per-file lock; commits move to a background WAL worker pool.

## Completion criteria

- `read_file` off the global lock.
- p99 write latency under 50ms at 21 clients.
- One faithful git commit per WAL entry.

# Web UI rebuild

**status:** active

Rebuild the Web UI to VS Code Web calibre: persistent docked shell, command palette, URL-as-state, dark design tokens. Client-only SPA behind the unchanged Go backend.

Phases: shell+read → live reactivity → editor → search+polish.

# Storage redesign — complete

**outcome:** shipped.

read_file p50 172ms → 1.1ms; p99 1895ms → 16.8ms; throughput 37.7 → 66.9 ops/sec. Mutex-contention delay 3394s → 2.25s. The compounding latency is gone.

# Web UI auto-refresh — complete

**outcome:** shipped.

`/ws/ui` now multiplexes NOTIFY events; views auto-refresh on storage writes, with reconnect + backoff. Verified end-to-end: `storage.Write` → NOTIFY → view refresh.

# Shoka — design

Design logs for Shoka. Each log is a dated pass; later passes may replace earlier conclusions while their evidence carries over.

# Catalog introduction

A per-project catalog makes listings fast and accurate without a git walk. Enables the instant-navigation and scoped-skeleton loading model the UI rebuild depends on.

# Web UI rebuild — third pass

Target: **VS Code Web's UI calibre** — command registry, keybinding service, docked persistent shell, URL-as-state + workbench-state, design tokens.

Recommended stack (from zero, by ecosystem fit): **React 19 + TanStack Router/Query + cmdk + CodeMirror 6 + react-arborist + react-markdown**, client-only SPA. Runner-up: SvelteKit-as-SPA.

The choice is contingent on the target, not on incumbency.

# Web UI redesign — first pass (replaced)

Compared Shoka to gekka-dashboard, concluded *reskin*. Rejected: read gekka's not-yet-built-out as not-needed.

# Rohrpost (dev)

Rohrpost is the first real client built on top of Shoka. This project holds its design log and working notes.

```sh
rohrpost serve --config config.yaml
```

# Rohrpost backlog

- [ ] Wire the MCP client to Shoka over Streamable HTTP.
- [ ] Skill export/install command.
- [x] Design log first draft.

Dogfooding Shoka as we go.

# Rohrpost design log

## Topology

iOS Claude app → Remote Control → `claude code` (per-project window) → Shoka MCP. Clients never connect to Shoka directly.

## Notes

Claude Desktop on macOS can connect to Shoka via `mcp-remote`, simplifying the meta-window role.

# Dogfooding notes — 2026-05-29

The directive-onboarding skill works: "execute latest directive" reads from Shoka, summarises, confirms, executes, writes reports back. This is the workflow we want.
